Commercial Mar 29, 2026

6 Commercial Aircraft With The Largest Wingspans Flying Today

The biggest wings in the sky in 2026.

Ultra-large wingspans optimize fuel efficiency and payload capacity, making them critical competitive advantages for long-haul and cargo operators in an era of rising fuel costs.

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Chaos Continues: TSA Callouts Reach Record Numbers As Over 500 Officers Quit

The recent departure of over 500 TSA officers has left security checkpoints nationwide grappling with worsening delays.

TSA staffing losses are creating operational vulnerabilities that could force airlines to adjust schedules or reduce flights during peak travel periods.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Many Airlines Choose Either Airbus Or Boeing

Both manufacturers have strengths, but there are benefits to standardization.

Fleet standardization reduces pilot training costs and maintenance complexity, making single-manufacturer commitments economically rational despite losing negotiating leverage.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Southwest Airlines Captain Flies Retirement Flight With First Officer Son

A Southwest captain’s final flight with his son highlights aviation family legacy, echoed by a similar emotional father-daughter story in Turkey.

Multi-generational aviation careers demonstrate the industry's ability to retain talent through family influence, offsetting pilot shortage pressures with organic recruitment.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to identifying and mitigating risks before they materialize, fundamentally changing safety culture.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Air Cargo Mar 29, 2026

Here’s How Much More Cargo The Boeing 747-8F Carries Compared To The 777F

The 747-8F and 777F represent the pinnacle of what Boeing currently offers to the cargo market. Find out what sets these two giants apart.

The 747-8F's significantly larger payload capacity justifies its higher operating costs for long-range cargo routes where volume efficiency directly impacts airline profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

How The Queen Of The Skies Made A Comeback At Delta Air Lines

The story behind the 747's last hurrah in the US.

Delta's 747 retirement marks the end of wide-body quad-jet operations for US carriers, signaling a permanent shift toward more fuel-efficient twin-engine aircraft for long-haul routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 29, 2026

Misawa’s 13th Fighter Squadron Receives First F-35s

Misawa Air Base has received the U.S. Air Force’s first F-35A Lightning II fighters to be permanently based in Japan, beginning the transition from the F-16 to the fifth-gen aircraft. Misawa Air Base, home of the U.S. Air Force’s 35th Fighter Wing, has received its first four F-35A Lightning II...

Japan's acquisition of permanent F-35 basing marks a strategic shift in U.S. forward-deployed capabilities in the Indo-Pacific region.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Do US Airlines Still Use The Boeing 757 On Key Routes?

Dozens of 757s still fly to this day, but four decades after the aircraft's introduction, why is this?

The 757's unmatched range, payload, and single-aisle efficiency on transcontinental and transatlantic routes justify its continued operation despite aging, as no modern replacement delivers the same capability-to-cost ratio.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

SMS effectiveness depends less on compliance procedures than on psychological safety that encourages frontline workers to report hazards without fear of retaliation.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Military Mar 29, 2026

Tight Space: Inside The B-2 Spirit Cockpit

The B-2 Spirit has to perform extra-long missions, while providing less space and comfort for its pilots than transport aircraft like the C-17.

B-2 pilots endure cramped cockpit conditions during multi-day missions, highlighting the operational trade-offs between stealth design and crew ergonomics in ultra-long-range platforms.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi

Aer Lingus has equipped the first aircraft in its fleet with Starlink, delivering ultrafast, reliable Wi-Fi to customers and marking a major step forward for customer experience. Customers travelling from Dublin to New York JFK on flight EI105 today are the first to experience the innovative...

Aer Lingus's Starlink integration enables competitive inflight connectivity without ground infrastructure dependency, positioning satellite-based systems as viable alternatives to traditional air-to-ground networks.

See Runway Girl Network →

Unidentified Drones Crash Near Kouvola, Finland, Authorities Investigate Possible Origin

Finnish authorities are investigating reports that one or two unmanned aerial systems havecrashed in southeastern...

Unexplained drone incidents in NATO-member Finland suggest emerging security vulnerabilities in European airspace requiring coordinated international response protocols.

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Op-Ed: Seating architecture must evolve to accommodate power, IFEC

Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Unum VP Business Development, Alan McInnes Moving beyond the traditional considerations of structure, comfort and certification, aircraft seating is a critical integration point for power, in-flight entertainment (IFE) and connectivity systems that...

Modern aircraft seats are becoming critical infrastructure nodes where power delivery and connectivity systems must be engineered alongside structural design, fundamentally changing cabin product development.

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design at major hubs can create pilot confusion during high-traffic operations, necessitating enhanced situational awareness and standardized taxiway signage protocols.

Premium economy's profitability hinges on load factor optimization rather than seat count, as airlines balance capacity constraints against yield management.

They include four new cities, with one in particular being historic...

Southwest's expansion into four new markets signals the carrier's confidence in point-to-point demand recovery beyond its traditional network footprint.

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

India inaugurates Noida International Airport, Delhi’s second gateway

India opened the first phase of Noida International Airport (DXN) in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, on March 28, 2026,…...

India's second Delhi-area airport reduces congestion at Indira Gandhi International, enabling capacity growth for the country's fastest-growing aviation market.

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS damaged in Iranian strike on Saudi air base

An Iranian missile and drone attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 27, 2026,…...

Loss of airborne early warning capability from AWACS damage creates critical detection gaps across regional air operations and highlights vulnerability of large, slow aircraft to modern air defenses.

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures require pilots to navigate complex terrain and class B airspace coordination, demanding precise execution to maintain community relations in congested Bay Area airspace.

Aerospace Mar 29, 2026

‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings

While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.

NASA's acceleration focus on robotic lunar missions reveals a strategic decoupling from human landing timelines, suggesting prolonged delays in crewed Artemis operations.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 29, 2026

USAF A-10 strike on Iraqi military base kills seven soldiers

An airstrike attributed to a US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II hit a military clinic at the Habbaniyah…...

Close-air-support incidents underscore escalating risks of friendly-fire casualties in complex multi-national combat zones with overlapping operational areas.

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of next-generation air-to-air missile systems suggests accelerated development timelines for beyond-visual-range combat capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission

Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

On-orbit servicing startups face execution risks when partnerships collapse, potentially delaying technology validation critical for future satellite constellation maintenance.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 29, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments offers a more immediate efficiency path than technology alone, requiring coordinated changes in airline scheduling, airport management, and air traffic control procedures.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...

Germany's potential adoption of the MQ-28A Ghost Bat signals NATO's shift toward integrating autonomous combat support aircraft with existing fighter fleets, addressing capability gaps without requiring new manned platforms.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 28, 2026

USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...

The USMC's F-35C deployment to the Middle East demonstrates carrier-based stealth fighter integration with amphibious operations during sustained regional conflict.

See The Aviationist →

Robinson Unmanned (formerly Ascent AeroSystems) is the uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) business unit of Robinson...

Robinson's pivot to autonomous systems positions the helicopter manufacturer to capitalize on UAS market growth while leveraging existing rotorcraft expertise.

Quantum Systems Strengthens Ukraine’s Air Defence Capabilities with 15,000 Interceptor Contract and WIY Drones Investment

In alignment with the defence priorities set out by the Ukrainian Government, Quantum Systems has...

Commercial drone manufacturer Quantum Systems is diversifying into military defense systems, signaling how commercial UAS technology is becoming critical infrastructure for national defense.

Singapore delays SAF levy rollout amid Middle East conflict

As the war in Iran continues to roil world oil markets, Singapore has delayed the rollout of its…...

Geopolitical oil market volatility is forcing governments to recalibrate aviation fuel policy implementation timelines, signaling broader economic pressure on airline operations.

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC

The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.

Artemis 2's compressed timeline from arrival to launch demonstrates NASA's operational readiness to return humans to lunar orbit after a decade-long gap in deep space exploration capability.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

[Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry

Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....

Damage to KC-135 tankers compromises critical aerial refueling capacity for U.S. regional operations, potentially degrading sortie range and mission sustainability.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

Listen to this audio excerpt from Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager: For Erik Richards, supporting NASA’s first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back is the culmination of a career spent helping spacecraft communicate with Earth. Like many kids who grew up at the height of the...

Deep space communication infrastructure, critical for Artemis lunar missions, relies on specialized expertise built over decades of spacecraft tracking and data relay operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...

NASA's $180.4M contract with Intuitive Machines accelerates commercial lunar logistics capabilities essential for sustained Artemis surface operations and deep-space exploration infrastructure.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories

Category I: NASA Environmental Quality Award Recognizes excellence in environmental management and planning, including stewardship of natural and cultural resources. This category highlights achievements in compliance, conservation, remediation, communication, and environmental information...

NASA's environmental awards program incentivizes aerospace contractors and centers to embed sustainability practices into facility operations, potentially influencing how aviation industry partners approach their own environmental compliance strategies.

See NASA →

EASA warns non-airworthy stolen engine parts could re-enter the market

The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) is warning airlines, maintenance providers and parts buyers to be on…...

Supply chain vigilance is critical as stolen engine components risk circumventing certification records, potentially compromising fleet safety and regulatory compliance across European operators.
